# True Non-Profit

True Non-Profit is a public knowledge book about non-profits, public-benefit institutions, and the assessment of how true a non-profit is to its stated mission.

Start here:

- /books/true-non-profit/index.html
- /books/true-non-profit/first-principles.html
- /books/true-non-profit/trueness-scale.html
- /books/true-non-profit/assessment-method.html
- /books/true-non-profit/register.html
- /books/true-non-profit/register.json
- /books/true-non-profit/assessment-schema.json

Core concept:

The trueness of a non-profit is the distance between its formal non-profit status and its actual behavior as a mission-governed, public-benefit institution.

Assessment status:

The public register is a scaffold. Real organizations should not receive scores until an evidence file exists with public source links, method notes, and reviewer status.
